rag_rules: When AGREE diagnostics report unresolved references or type errors, trace the root cause to invalid identifiers or mismatched types in the same line and fix or remove the offending clause.
For implementation-level AGREE contracts, use assign only for output features or declared auxiliary variables; avoid placing guarantees in implementation scope.
If AGREE reports type mismatches like '<error>' or '<error> and int', ensure all identifiers are properly resolved and types match in binary expressions.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must reference only visible AADL features or declared helpers within the same annex scope.
AGREE scalar types must be int, real, or bool; avoid package-qualified or invalid type names.
When a guarantee or equation expression evaluates to an error type, remove or correct the clause to align with valid AADL types.
Assignments in implementation scope must target existing output ports or declared auxiliary variables.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type annexes are for assumptions/guarantees; implementation annexes are for bindings and assignments.
Use only visible identifiers or declared eq variables in AGREE expressions to avoid '<error>' type issues.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or types not present in the AADL model's configured dependencies.
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope; use only assignments and equations there.
Do not attempt to fix type errors by adding new architecture; instead, correct or remove invalid clauses.
